title: Playbook - PowerShell Security Data Pipeline: Qualys / BitSight / Defender → SharePoint → Power BI
applies_to: End-to-end PowerShell pipeline combining Qualys VMDR, BitSight ratings, and Defender for Endpoint
Staging security data to SharePoint lists and triggering Power BI refresh
Azure Automation runbook orchestrating the full multi-source reporting cycle
prerequisites: App registration in Entra ID with Sites.ReadWrite.All (Graph), SecurityAlert.Read.All, Machine.Read.All application permissions
Azure Automation Account with System-assigned Managed Identity (or certificate-based service principal) granted the above permissions
Modules imported into Automation Account: PnP.PowerShell, ImportExcel (or use Graph SDK for SharePoint writes)
Qualys API credentials (username + password) stored in Automation Account encrypted variables or Key Vault
BitSight API token stored in Automation Account encrypted variable or Key Vault
SharePoint site with staging lists: QualysHostDetections, BitSightRatings, DefenderDevices — each with a SourceKey indexed column
Power BI dataset configured to use SharePoint lists or uploaded CSV files as data source

request_sequence.name: Authenticate
request_sequence.description: Establish all API connections at script start. For Azure Automation with Managed Identity: Connect-AzAccount -Identity; Connect-MgGraph -Identity; Connect-PnPOnline -Url $spSiteUrl -ManagedIdentity. Retrieve Qualys and BitSight credentials from Key Vault or Automation encrypted variables.
request_sequence.error_handling: If any connection fails, write structured error to Output stream and exit. Do not proceed with partial authentication.

request_sequence.name: Pre-load SharePoint staging list maps
request_sequence.description: For each staging list (QualysHostDetections, BitSightRatings, DefenderDevices), load all existing items into a hashtable keyed by SourceKey. Pattern: $existingMap = @{}; Get-PnPListItem -List 'QualysHostDetections' -PageSize 500 | ForEach-Object { $existingMap[$_.FieldValues['SourceKey']] = $_.Id }. This enables O(1) upsert lookups without per-row CAML queries.
request_sequence.error_handling: Log item counts loaded. If list is empty, proceed — first run will be all inserts.

request_sequence.name: Extract Qualys VMDR host detections
request_sequence.description: Call Qualys VMDR detection API with truncation_limit=1000 and pagination via id_min. For each host, aggregate detection counts by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low). Build a PSCustomObject array with HostId, IPAddress, HostName, LastScanDate, CriticalCount, HighCount, MediumCount, LastUpdated.
request_sequence.error_handling: Wrap each API call in Invoke-ApiRequest with retry logic. On persistent failure after 5 attempts, skip Qualys step and log warning; continue with BitSight and Defender.

request_sequence.name: Extract BitSight portfolio ratings
request_sequence.description: Call GET /ratings/v1/companies to get all portfolio companies. For each company, call GET /ratings/v1/companies/{guid} for factor breakdown. Build PSCustomObject array with CompanyGuid, CompanyName, OverallRating, RatingDate, and per-category ratings (CompromisedSystems, Diligence, UserBehavior, etc.).
request_sequence.error_handling: Add 250ms delay between per-company detail calls. On 429, apply Retry-After backoff. On persistent failure, skip BitSight step and log warning.

request_sequence.name: Extract Defender for Endpoint device inventory
request_sequence.description: Call GET https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com/api/machines via Invoke-RestMethod with Graph or Defender token. Paginate using @odata.nextLink. Build PSCustomObject array with DeviceId, DeviceName, RiskScore, ExposureLevel, OSPlatform, LastSeen, OnboardingStatus.
request_sequence.error_handling: Use Graph token with Machine.Read.All permission. On throttle (429), honor Retry-After. Log device count extracted.

request_sequence.name: Upsert all data to SharePoint staging lists
request_sequence.description: For each data source, apply upsert logic using the pre-loaded existingMap from step 2. Build separate insert and update lists. Execute inserts in PnP batches of 100. Execute updates individually (PnP batch updates may have limitations). Set LastUpdated = Get-Date -Format 'o' (UTC ISO 8601) on every row.
request_sequence.error_handling: Log insert and update counts per list. On batch failure, retry the failed batch once. Log any items that could not be written.

request_sequence.name: Generate Excel and HTML summary reports
request_sequence.description: Using ImportExcel, create a workbook with sheets: Executive Summary (KPI counts), Qualys Detail, BitSight Detail, Defender Detail. Apply conditional formatting for critical counts > 0 (red) and ratings < 700 (orange). Using PSWriteHTML, generate an HTML summary for email distribution.
request_sequence.error_handling: Report generation is non-blocking. If it fails, log warning but do not halt pipeline — data is already staged to SharePoint.

request_sequence.name: Upload reports to SharePoint and trigger Power BI refresh
request_sequence.description: Upload the Excel report to the SharePoint document library: Add-PnPFile -Path $reportPath -Folder 'Shared Documents/Reports'. Then trigger Power BI dataset refresh: POST to https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/datasets/{datasetId}/refreshes with a Power BI bearer token. Poll refresh status until Completed or Failed (max 30 minutes).
request_sequence.error_handling: On Power BI refresh failure, log the error details from the refresh history response. Do not retry more than once.

request_sequence.name: Send summary email
request_sequence.description: Use Send-MgUserMail to send the HTML summary and attach the Excel report. Include KPI summary in the email body: total devices by risk score, average BitSight rating, critical Qualys findings count. Recipients from a configuration variable.
request_sequence.error_handling: Email failure is non-blocking. Log warning and exit cleanly.

request_sequence.name: Log completion and disconnect
request_sequence.description: Write structured completion log to Output stream: start time, end time, records extracted per source, records inserted/updated per list, Power BI refresh status. Call Disconnect-PnPOnline. Disconnect-MgGraph. This creates a clean audit trail in Azure Automation job history.
request_sequence.error_handling: Always execute this step even if prior steps had partial failures.

implementation_notes: Run the pipeline daily at 06:00 UTC via Azure Automation schedule. This ensures Power BI reports are current before business hours in most timezones.
The pipeline is idempotent by design: re-running it on the same day updates existing SharePoint records (via upsert) and re-uploads the report file. Repeated runs do not create duplicates.
For the first run on a new environment, skip the Power BI refresh trigger until the semantic model is validated against the initial data load.

Keep each step in a separate function to enable independent testing and debugging. The step sequence above maps directly to function names: Invoke-QualysExtract, Invoke-BitSightExtract, Invoke-DefenderExtract, Invoke-SharePointUpsert, etc.

For the Executive Summary worksheet, calculate KPIs in PowerShell before writing: total critical findings = ($qualysData | Measure-Object CriticalCount -Sum).Sum; average BitSight rating = ($bitsightData | Measure-Object OverallRating -Average).Average; high-risk device count = ($defenderData | Where-Object RiskScore -eq 'High').Count.
caveats: Defender for Endpoint API uses a separate token endpoint and permission scope from Microsoft Graph. Acquire it separately from the Graph token.

Azure Automation sandbox has a 3-hour job timeout. The full pipeline should complete in under 30 minutes for typical portfolio sizes (< 500 Qualys hosts, < 100 BitSight companies, < 10,000 Defender devices).
Power BI dataset refresh count limits apply (8/day for Pro, 48/day for Premium). Avoid triggering more than once per pipeline run.
SharePoint list view threshold: ensure SourceKey column is indexed before the list exceeds 5,000 items. For Defender devices (potentially >10,000), use a dedicated list with index.

BitSight per-company factor detail calls multiply quickly: 100 companies = 101 API calls. With 250ms delay this takes ~25 seconds. For portfolios >500 companies, consider caching factor data and only refreshing companies where the rating changed.
